text: This is Dr. Craig Rogers, Director of Renal Surgery at the Vatakuta Urology Institute at Henry Ford Hospital.
The surgeon reconstructs the inner layer of the resection bed, focusing on the precise alignment and suturing of the tissue layers.
The surgeon exchanges the robotic instruments for a robotic needle driver, preparing for suturing or tissue manipulation within the surgical field.
The surgeon uses the ProGrasp or fenestrated bipolar instruments in the left hand as a needle driver, demonstrating the versatility of these robotic tools during the nephrectomy procedure.
The surgeon uses a 3-0 Monocryl suture on an SH needle to reconstruct the inner layer of the resection bed during the nephrectomy procedure.
The surgeon anchors all sutures with a knot and secures them using a hemlock clip, ensuring stability in the reconstructed inner layer of the resection bed.
The surgeon passes the suture through the renal capsule into the defect using a robotic needle driver, preparing for inner layer reconstruction.
The surgeon anchors a hemlock clip onto the renal capsule using robotic instruments, securing the suture in place.
The surgeon performs continuous suturing of the resection bed, closing small vessels and repairing any entries into the collecting system using a suture technique.
Other suture options such as forovicral, barb suture, smaller needles, and suturing of individual vessels can also be used per surgeon preference.
The surgeon carefully places sutures through the renal capsule, ensuring they are not too deep to prevent vascular injury, while avoiding the collecting system or pseudoaneurysm.
The surgeon completes suturing the inner layer and passes the needle through the renal capsule to the outside of the kidney, securing the outer layer.
The assistant places a hemlock clip on the renal capsule to secure the stitch, ensuring stability of the sutured area.
The surgeon places additional sutures in the resection bed to occlude small bleeding vessels, ensuring hemostasis in the surgical field.
The surgeon focuses on reconstructing the inner layer of the kidney to achieve hemostasis during renorraphy, ensuring proper closure and control of bleeding.
In the next video, we will demonstrate the technique for sutured closure of the renal capsule.
